description: Incremental refresh policy for large fact tables migrated from Crystal Reports and SSRS into the consolidated Azure SQL data source.

RangeStart.description: Start of the data range window. Managed automatically by the Power BI service during refresh.

RangeEnd.description: End of the data range window. Managed automatically by the Power BI service during refresh.

historical.description: Store 2 full years of historical data in import partitions.

realTime.description: Current-year data served via DirectQuery for real-time freshness.

incrementalRefreshWindow.description: Only refresh the most recent 10 days of data during each incremental refresh cycle.

detectDataChanges.description: Only refresh partitions where modified_date has a newer value than the last recorded refresh timestamp.

sourceFilter.description: Applied as a native query filter to limit rows fetched from Azure SQL.

historical.description: Store 2 full years of historical inventory snapshots.

incrementalRefreshWindow.description: Refresh the most recent 7 days during each incremental refresh.

prerequisites: RangeStart and RangeEnd must be defined as DateTime parameters in the Power Query editor.
The source query must filter on these parameters for incremental refresh to take effect.
The modified_date column must be indexed in Azure SQL for efficient change detection.
bestPractices: Keep the incremental window small to minimize refresh duration.
Use DirectQuery for real-time partitions only if the Azure SQL tier supports the query load.
Monitor partition counts; excessive granularity can degrade model performance.
